key_risks: A hotter-than-expected jobs report or ISM services prices reading could push yields higher and trigger a reversal in expensive growth and AI equities.
Recent extreme momentum in South Korea, solar, software, and AI themes could mean crowded positioning and abrupt profit-taking during the scoring week.
A risk-off move from weak labor data interpreted as recessionary rather than rate-friendly would likely hurt high-beta thematic equities more than the S&P 500.
Semiconductor or AI-leadership rotation into defensive or value sectors could cause concentrated underperformance versus the benchmark.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest near-term thematic setup from AI spending forecasts and recent price momentum, with broad exposure across AI infrastructure and applications.
Software had exceptional 7-day and 30-day momentum and may benefit if upcoming labor/services data lowers rate pressure on growth equities.
Global semiconductor sales growth and AI infrastructure capex support continued leadership in chip-related equities over a short horizon.
Very strong trailing momentum and heavy semiconductor/export sensitivity offer high beta to the same AI and chip cycle leadership.
Recent breakout momentum is the strongest in the universe, and a small allocation captures upside while limiting idiosyncratic clean-energy volatility.

portfolio_rationale: The portfolio intentionally concentrates in the highest-momentum AI, software, semiconductor, and related high-beta exposures because one-week relative performance is often dominated by trend persistence and risk appetite. Scheduled ISM services and payrolls could also support growth equities if they ease rate concerns without signaling a sharp downturn.
rationale_summary: This is an aggressive one-week alpha portfolio tilted toward AI/technology momentum plus selective high-beta satellite exposure. It aims to beat the S&P 500 through continued leadership in software, chips, Korean tech, and solar rather than broad diversification.
